name: The Night Cafe: An Immersive Tribute to Van Gogh

reviews.review_description: You really feel like you're inside a van Gogh. Superb use of gear vr. I hope to see an entire virtual museum stylized in this way one day.
This is AMAZING! Exploring the back hallway and the side room is quite interesting. Be sure and look out the window beyond the piano!
We need more apps like this.
Please, please, please create a similar one!! This was a wonderful experience and so relaxing! Thank you!!
Please make it "walk" slowly so my friends won't get sick. Otherwise, fantastic app.
Nice show off to artist friends app
Literally mind blowing! I whish that there will be more places to go but other than that it's totally beautiful

From the very first moment I was hooked. This was my first experience of VR and the emersion was unbelievable. I do hope you create further works from Vincents paintings and I await your next project.
I hv enteres to the world of art. I felt nauseous after a while though.
Not working on 8+. Please fix

It is amazing. I just wish you could "visit" more paintings and explore the existing environment more (i.e. open doors, go downstairs). It is very surreal but not frightening. Feels very real when you're standing.
A very nice concept! It looks great. Really hoping for more content in the future!
Used to love it on my S6, unfortunately it doesn't work on S8. Anyway, without being an art fan very enjoyable app.

I dont know how old this painting is but being able to ttansport the user inside of it, is nothing short of amazing. The point is... you're immersed inside a world that existed only in the painters head! WTF could be more transformative?!? This is like a museum on crack, albeit a small experience, i appreciate what the developers have done to bring this virtual work of art to my face. Thank you!

great way to see art ..... this is the only vr I've used that's given motion sickness apart from that great ... I'm wondering if it's sitting down and using the controls on oculus vr or just the controls moving too fast ... exllent otherwise
I love this so much.  Beautiful

Bravo. What a stunning experience. This is a truly wonderful use of VR. Equal parts mesmerising, intimate and disconcerting, with a weird sense of melancholy, it's something that will stick with you long after you take the headset off. Like others have said, a full, open world with all his paintings interconnected and explorable seems like the logical next step and would probably change people's lives! Here's hoping that comes out on the next gen.
Nicely done. I really enjoyed this. Adds another experience as you walk around.
Would love to explore more art like this if they improved the comfort

The Night Cafe is definitely something you can use to wow family and friends. The "living painting" environment is incredibly stunning and the music sets the mood wonderfully. I've already played this 5 times and it remains just as fascinating as the first time. there were more experiences like this on the gear vr!
